title: From The Temporary To The Eternal Lessons From The Life Of The Apostle Paul
scriptureForWeek: Several sections of scripture highlighting Paul’s teaching on the eternal consequences of what happens when people reject Christ.
weekTheme: Boys mock; Men weep God will judge evil Where do you go when you die? Have you ever really given that any thought? Almost all religions and philosophies teach that something happens when we die. These teachings fall into two categories: goodness for the righteous and judgment for the wicked. This week we will be looking at a really sober subject, and one that guys your age tragically give little thought to. It’s the concept of eternal punishment. It's important that you develop the right convictions on this, as your view of the future will shape the kind of man you become over the course of your life. You have to discern the truth about this because there are so many opinions floating around our world today. Some religions teach reincarnation - that you come back and live another life based on how you performed in this one. It’s an endless cycle of trying to continually improve into enlightenment. Some teach universalism - that we all live in a place of light, life, and peace in a spiritual realm, and everyone will get there regardless of what they have done. Some teach salvation by works - that those who are good go somewhere good, and the bad go to hell. This is just scratching the surface. There are dozens of different views about what happens when we die. They all have one thing in common that's important

days.title: Rejecting The Big Lie (Mtd)
days.scripture: 1 CORINTHIANS 6:9-11 Don’t you realize that those who do wrong will not inherit the Kingdom of God? Don’t fool yourselves. Those who indulge in sexual sin, or who worship idols, or commit adultery, or are male prostitutes, or practice homosexuality, or are thieves, or greedy people, or drunkards, or are abusive, or cheat people— none of these will inherit the Kingdom of God. Some of you were once like that. But you were cleansed; you were made holy; you were made right with God by calling on the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
days.reflection: There is a concept floating around in our world today called Moral Therapeutic Deism. What a weird set of words. What on earth do they mean? How could they have anything to do with your life? The answer is a lot. MTD (you may remember we have touched on this before in The Primal Path) is the way most people your age think about God, religion, heaven, and hell. It’s based on what humans wish was true, not what God has revealed to us is actually true. Here are the five core beliefs: 1.A God exists who created and ordered the world and watches over human life on earth. 2.God wants people to be good, nice, and fair to each other, as taught in the Bible and by most world religions. 3.The central goal of life is to be happy and to feel good about oneself. 4.God does not need to be particularly involved in one's life except when God is needed to resolve a problem. 5.Good people go to heaven when they die. When you read those initially, there is some truth in them. God did make the world. God does value kindness. But there are some massive errors in there. The goal of life isn’t to be happy. God doesn’t exist to drop in and show up like a genie. Getting to heaven isn’t about being good (because none of us would make it), and all religions teach different core tenants. You can see that MTD is how most people think today because it shows up in the way they react to truth. It shows up in how your friends respond, teachers respond, media personalities respond, and celebrities respond. It sounds like this: “I thought that God was only about love and not judgment.” “What gives you the right to think your opinion is the right one?” “Live your truth” “Be your authentic self” “I don’t think a loving God would ever send someone to hell” In today’s reading, the Apostle Paul wanted the Corinthians not to get sucked into the lies their culture was telling about eternal punishment. He wrote: Don’t you realize that those who do wrong will not inherit the Kingdom of God? Don’t fool yourselves. We live in a world that fools itself all the time. It says that we can do what we want, say what we want, spend what we want, have sex with who we want, and hate what we want without any sort of consequences. All you have to do is look at our society to see that this isn’t how life works. There are consequences to every choice and every action. Even you know that from your experience growing up. Paul gives a pretty gnarly list of people who won’t inherit the kingdom of God, then warns the Corinthians not to get fooled by human opinions. The thing is, because it's God's kingdom that we are invited into, He gets to determine the rules. He gets to set the boundaries and determine what’s best for us. The good news of Paul’s message, though, ends with this: Even though you used to do things that kept you out of God's kingdom, Jesus has come to love, save and change you. His grace is stronger than our sin. Look at how cool this verse is. But you were cleansed; you were made holy; you were made right with God by calling on the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God. Cleansed. Made Holy. Made right with God. Given the Holy Spirit. This mercy and love is better than any sin. Don’t be deceived. Sin has consequences. Sin shuts you out. Sin will be punished. But don’t lose hope. You are loved. God will forgive you. He can cleanse and change you.
questions: Can you think of an example from our culture you have seen recently where someone expressed one of the five ideas of MTD? What did they say? Which one was it?

Where do you think guys your age tend to get fooled into thinking they can do what they want and get away with it? Can sin be without consequences? What advice could you give to show that it’s not true?

Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they have experienced Jesus cleansing them, making them holy, making them right with God, and being filled with the Holy Spirit. Have them be as specific as possible.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Hear my cry, O Lord. Pay attention to my prayer. LORD, if you kept a record of our sins, who, O Lord, could ever survive? But you offer forgiveness, that we might learn to fear you. Psalms 130:2-4 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to take away any foolish perspectives that may have crept into your thinking and renew your mind with truth. Thank Him for His love and forgiveness that saves us from sin and eternal judgment.

days.title: Rejecting Mercy
days.scripture: 2 THESSALONIANS 1:7-10 And God will provide rest for you who are being persecuted and also for us when the Lord Jesus appears from heaven. He will come with his mighty angels, in flaming fire, bringing judgment on those who don’t know God and on those who refuse to obey the Good News of our Lord Jesus. They will be punished with eternal destruction, forever separated from the Lord and from his glorious power. When he comes on that day, he will receive glory from his holy people—praise from all who believe. And this includes you, for you believed what we told you about him.
days.reflection: People don’t go to hell because God wants them to. The Bible couldn’t be any more clear about that. God's heart is that everyone would be saved. Look at what these verses say: "The Lord isn’t really being slow about his promise, as some people think. No, he is being patient for your sake. He does not want anyone to be destroyed, but wants everyone to repent." 2 Peter 3:9 "God sent his Son into the world not to judge the world, but to save the world through him." John 3:17 So why would anyone face eternal judgment? People go to hell because they refuse to accept God’s grace. They resist God, reject His mercy, and refuse His pardon. It may sound crazy that anyone would reject the invitation of salvation that God offers, but it's true; people do all the time. There is a famous incident in history that illustrates this. In 1829, a man named George Wilson was arrested for robbery and murder in a US mail holdup. He was tried, convicted, and sentenced to death by hanging. Some friends intervened on his behalf and were able to obtain his pardon from President Andrew Jackson. But Wilson refused it, saying he wanted to die. Well, the sheriff didn’t know what to do; how do you execute a man officially pardoned? An appeal was made to the President. Being perplexed, he turned the matter over to the US Supreme Court. Chief Justice John Marshall gave this ruling: A pardon is a piece of paper, the value of which depends on its acceptance by the person implicated. Thus, George Wilson was executed while his pardon lay on the sheriff’s desk! Sometimes people paint God as an angry person sitting on His thrown trying to catch people so He can punish them. But it's actually quite the opposite. God is slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love. He loves to show mercy. But people reject that. Those who refuse to obey the Good News of our Lord Jesus Refusing the good news has heartbreaking consequences... Judgment Punishment Eternal separation Eternal destruction Read those words slowly and let them sink in. As a young man learning to live in the world, heed Jesus' invitation and warning. Listen to His pleasing heart of love. He does not want you to perish. He does not want eternal judgment, punishment, separation, and destruction for you. Find rest in His heart. Find rest in His mercy. Choose to obey the good news of the gospel of Jesus and live.
questions: What are some reasons that guys your age resist the mercy of Jesus and the good news of His love?
Do you think this verse really means what it says or not? If so, what do you think your response should be?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share about a time they saw someone turn from their sins and accept God's mercy. Who was it, what happened, and how did they change?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. For this is how God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life. God sent his Son into the world not to judge the world, but to save the world through him. “There is no judgment against anyone who believes in him. But anyone who does not believe in him has already been judged for not believing in God’s one and only Son. And the judgment is based on this fact: God’s light came into the world, but people loved the darkness more than the light, for their actions were evil. All who do evil hate the light and refuse to go near it for fear their sins will be exposed.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: If you have never given your life to Jesus and responded to the gospel, you can do that right now. Just ask Him to forgive your sin because of what Jesus did on the cross. Offer your life to Him and invite Him to lead your life.

days.title: Resisting Rebellion
days.scripture: ROMANS 1:18,28-32 The wrath of God is being revealed from heaven against all the godlessness and wickedness of people, who suppress the truth by their wickedness. Since they thought it foolish to acknowledge God, he abandoned them to their foolish thinking and let them do things that should never be done. Their lives became full of every kind of wickedness, sin, greed, hate, envy, murder, quarreling, deception, malicious behavior, and gossip. They are backstabbers, haters of God, insolent, proud, and boastful. They invent new ways of sinning, and they disobey their parents. They refuse to understand, break their promises, are heartless, and have no mercy. They know God’s justice requires that those who do these things deserve to die, yet they do them anyway. Worse yet, they encourage others to do them, too.
days.reflection: Do you think people are good or bad by nature? It’s a hard question to answer isn’t it? Sometimes we see people do things that are incredibly kind and compassionate. It makes us remember how amazing people can be. Then we see them do horrific things. From bullying to murder, people are capable of incredible cruelty. Hitler and Mother Teresa are part of the same human family. You probably feel both of these forces in your own life too. Sometimes you do kind things, and other times you probably sense selfishness and an intense desire for what you know is wrong, which is kind of scary. Contrary to popular culture, the Bible teaches that people have a bent toward evil, not a bent toward good. We are all made in God's image, so we still retain our sense of honor and dignity, but we are bent and broken by sin. It’s a result of the fall of Adam and Eve and their desire to try and live apart from God's guidance and wisdom. Saint Augustine said we are all born with our backs turned towards God. This places us, by default, as children of wrath and not children of God. Paul says this to the church in Ephesus about who we were before Jesus saved us: "Once you were dead because of your disobedience and your many sins. You used to live in sin, just like the rest of the world, obeying the devil—the commander of the powers in the unseen world. He is the spirit at work in the hearts of those who refuse to obey God. All of us used to live that way, following the passionate desires and inclinations of our sinful nature. By our very nature we were subject to God’s anger, just like everyone else." Ephesians 2:1-3 British pastor N.T. Wright said, "Evil is what happens when humans, given the task of looking after God's world, choose to worship that which is not God." When we turn away from God, things go bad quickly. This is where the evil rushes in. This is what Paul was warning the church in Rome about in today’s reading. This is what happens when people rebel against God. When a kid acts out in class, the teacher takes that kid out of class so they don’t disrupt the learning of the other students. When men act out in society and break the law, the courts put this person in prison so they don’t hurt people in society. God will do this when people rebel against Him and refuse to live according to His life-giving commands. He will take them from heaven and put them in hell. It even says this in the book of Revelation three times!!! “But the cowardly, the unbelieving, the vile, the murderers, the sexually immoral, those who practice magic arts, the idolaters, and all liars--they will be consigned to the fiery lake of burning sulfur. This is the second death.” Revelation 21:8 “Nothing impure will ever enter it, nor will anyone who does what is shameful or deceitful, but only those whose names are written in the Lamb’s book of life.” Revelation 21:27 “Blessed are those who wash their robes, that they may have the right to the tree of life and may go through the gates into the city. Outside are the dogs, those who practice magic arts, the sexually immoral, the murderers, the idolaters, and everyone who loves and practices falsehood.” Revelation 22:14-15 God isn’t doing this because He hates these people. He is doing this to protect the people of the new creation and the new world He will make. He wants everyone to come in, but some refuse. As a result, they are kept out. God is radically committed to helping you grow into a man who brings heaven to earth, not unleashes hell. He wants to keep the evil out of your life and use you to keep the evil out of our world. When men rebel against God, they suffer, and everything they touch does as well. When men follow God, they flourish, and what they touch does too. Make it the deepest vision of your heart to be inside God's new creation; inside the city of God. Resolve in your heart to resist rebellion; to be a man who brings good into the world by the grace of Jesus. Eternity is at stake.
questions: Of all the things listed as rebellion to God in today’s reading, which ones stand out the most to you? Why?
Can you list some examples where you see our culture rebelling against God? What are some ways they have “invented new ways to sin” that you can think of?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how God convicted them of their rebellion against God and turned their hearts towards him.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Once we, too, were foolish and disobedient. We were misled and became slaves to many lusts and pleasures. Our lives were full of evil and envy, and we hated each other. But when God our Savior revealed his kindness and love, he saved us, not because of the righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He washed away our sins, giving us a new birth and new life through the Holy Spirit. He generously poured out the Spirit upon us through Jesus Christ our Savior. Because of his grace he made us right in his sight and gave us confidence that we will inherit eternal life. Titus 3:3-7 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to remove any rebellion in your heart toward Him and His word.

days.title: Tears For Destruction
days.scripture: PHILLIPIANS 3:17-21 Join together in following my example, brothers and sisters, and just as you have us as a model, keep your eyes on those who live as we do. For, as I have often told you before and now tell you again even with tears, many live as enemies of the cross of Christ. Their destiny is destruction, their god is their stomach, and their glory is in their shame. Their mind is set on earthly things. But our citizenship is in heaven. And we eagerly await a Savior from there, the Lord Jesus Christ, who, by the power that enables him to bring everything under his control, will transform our lowly bodies so that they will be like his glorious body.
days.reflection: What’s the first thing that comes to mind when you think about hell? When the media portrays what hell is like, they give a totally distorted perspective. They paint hell as a place of fun and partying. People drinking, having fun, and doing all the naughty stuff people really want to do. Heaven, on the other hand, is presented as a boring place for boring people who dress in white and sing lame songs. This is a total lie. Hell is not a place of fun; it’s a place of torment. The Bible uses a series of really heavy words to talk about what happens to people when they die without being reconciled to God. They perish, they suffer, they are destroyed. They are in outer darkness. They go to a place prepared for the devil and his angels. They are accursed. The experience of this is also described in the most sober of terms: Weeping and gnashing of teeth. Wailing. Torment. A “fire that is not quenched.” When the New Testament talks about hell, it's referencing an actual place that existed in the city of Jerusalem. It was the garbage dump of the town. It was a valley called Gehenna, or Hinnom. At the time of Jesus and Paul, many Jewish people believed it to be the most unholy, god-forsaken place on earth. In the Old Testament, it was the place babies were sacrificed to pagan gods. God had forsaken and condemned the place. It was where all the garbage and refuse was thrown. This garbage was burned to destroy it, and often there were worms in the rubbish and fire was perpetually lit to destroy the rot. That’s what Jesus had in mind when he talked about hell. How horrific. How tragic. This is not a place of pleasure and partying, but one of horror and heartache. Death, trash, fire, abandonment by God. Think Mordor in Lord of the Rings, not spring break at the beach. When Paul sees people headed there, he weeps. He lists out a few core characteristics of people headed to hell. They are enemies of the cross They want nothing to do with Jesus' death for their sin. They are at war with it. They hate it. They view Jesus as their enemy. Their god is their belly They worship physical pleasure. They are driven by whatever their bodies want to do. Their mind is set on earthly things They only care about the here and now. Sex. Money, power, pleasure, ease, toys, hobbies. They have no vision of God's kingdom or love. They glory in their shame They are excited about the wrong things. They are celebrating what should be condemned. They have cut God out of their life. Their destiny is destruction They will not be allowed to wreak havoc in the world God is creating. They will face destruction. They will be shut out and excluded from the glorious future God has for the world. Paul’s response to this isn’t self-righteousness. He doesn’t get up on a moral high horse. He doesn’t think he is better than others because he knows Jesus and has accepted the truth. He weeps. He has a broken heart. The thought of hell should produce tears. Jesus wept over those who rejected Him. You are going to need to learn to do that as well.
questions: Can you think of a couple of examples in the media where hell is portrayed as fun and heaven as boring?
Which of the images of hell hits you the hardest? Why?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share about a time they felt a deep burden and heartache for someone who had rejected Jesus. What did God do in them? What did they do about it?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. I speak the truth in Christ—I am not lying, my conscience confirms it through the Holy Spirit—I have great sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart. For I could wish that I myself were cursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my people, those of my own race, four the people of Israel. Romans 9:1-4 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a broken heart for those who don’t know Jesus and are headed for destruction.

days.title: Warning
days.scripture: EPHESIANS 5:3-7 But among you there must not be even a hint of sexual immorality, or of any kind of impurity, or of greed, because these are improper for God’s holy people. Nor should there be obscenity, foolish talk or coarse joking, which are out of place, but rather thanksgiving. For of this you can be sure: No immoral, impure or greedy person—such a person is an idolater — has any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God. Let no one deceive you with empty words, for because of such things God’s wrath comes on those who are disobedient. Therefore, do not be partners with them.
days.reflection: What is the essence of the message we believe as Christians? Is it that everyone is ok and there is nothing to worry about? Is it that God can’t stand people and He is waiting for us to screw up and send us to hell? If you could sum up the whole Christian faith in just a couple of lines, what would they be? Tim Keller, one of the most thoughtful pastors of our time, was asked that and he said this: “The gospel is this: We are more sinful and flawed in ourselves than we ever dared believe, yet at the very same time, we are more loved and accepted in Jesus Christ than we ever dared hope.” This is a really cool summary of the message we hold to as believers. It has two parts. WE ARE MORE SINFUL THAN WE DARE BELIEVE Contrary to what the culture says, people are sinful. We aren’t just broken or sad or struggling; we are evil and live in a state of rebellion toward God. This means we are children of wrath. Without God, without hope, and without salvation. Left to ourselves, we will perish. Look at how Paul put it in today’s reading. For of this you can be sure: No immoral, impure or greedy person—such a person is an idolater —has any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God. Let no one deceive you with empty words, for because of such things God’s wrath comes on those who are disobedient. Therefore, do not be partners with them. Jesus warned that the consequences of rejecting God’s grace were disastrous. He says: “Don’t be afraid of those who want to kill your body; they cannot touch your soul. Fear only God, who can destroy both soul and body in hell." Matthew 10:28 When you read what hell is actually like in the book of Revelation, it’s absolutely sobering. "And I saw a great white throne and the one sitting on it. The earth and sky fled from his presence, but they found no place to hide. I saw the dead, both great and small, standing before God’s throne. And the books were opened, including the Book of Life. And the dead were judged according to what they had done, as recorded in the books. The sea gave up its dead, and death and the grave gave up their dead. And all were judged according to their deeds. Then death and the grave were thrown into the lake of fire. This lake of fire is the second death. And anyone whose name was not found recorded in the Book of Life was thrown into the lake of fire." Revelation 20:11-15 The end result for those who reject Christ is the lake of fire. The main point here is not that it’s a literal lake of fire. The main point is that it's horrific; people try and hide from God to escape, but they can’t, and this is called the second death. WE ARE MORE LOVED IN JESUS THAN WE EVER DARED HOPE But then comes the good news. The whole story of the Bible is that God wants a relationship with us. He continually pursues and provides for us. The good news of what He has done is called the gospel. The gospel is the good news about the life, death, and resurrection of Jesus on our behalf. He lived the life we couldn’t live, died the death we deserve, then rose again to give us eternal life. One of the most famous verses in the Bible makes this so clear. In light of all you have looked at this past week, hear it with fresh ears. “For this is how God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life. God sent his Son into the world not to judge the world, but to save the world through him." John 3:16-17 This week has been a heavy week. It’s been a week where you have considered some of the most important and serious things in life; your eternal destiny. You will die. You will stand before Jesus and give an account for your life. Accept His mercy and receive His love. He has done everything needed to take away your sin and forgive you. He loves you. He wants you to spend eternity with Him in the new heaven and new earth. Ask Him for His mercy and your name to be in the book of life. This is the time. Shift from being a boy who thinks about the temporary things to a man who lives for the eternal.
questions: Out of all the things you talked about this week, what stood out to you the most? Why?
What are one or two things you could do each week to keep your mind focused on the big picture of God's story and not just get distracted by what’s happening around you?

If you have any thoughts, questions, or things you want to talk about regarding the topic of God's anger, judgment, wrath, or hell, ask the person you are doing this study with. Feel free to be totally honest and talk about anything you want.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. These are some of the most clear verses in the Bible about sin, redemption, and our call to share the good news with others. All this is from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and gave us the ministry of reconciliation: that God was reconciling the world to himself in Christ, not counting people’s sins against them. And he has committed to us the message of reconciliation. We are therefore Christ’s ambassadors, as though God were making his appeal through us. We implore you on Christ’s behalf: Be reconciled to God. God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God. 2 Corinthians 5:18-21 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for His gift of salvation. Then take a moment to pray for your friends by name who do not know Jesus.

extras: WEEK 7 MANSCHOOL STATE OF THE SOUL MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. STATE OF THE SOUL We are coming toward the close of the first year of the Primal Path. Can you believe it? In the midst of everything going on, why not just sit down for a good conversation with your son to check in and see how he is doing? How have things been going for him? What’s causing him stress? You can do this any way that feels right to you. Over a game where you check in with a couple of questions. Going for a hike or out fishing. Over a firepit. Just someplace you won’t get distracted. It may not feel like it now, but he will be very grateful for a dad who checked in on his heart. OPTION 2. WATCH THE MATRIX The world is trying to present a view of reality that blinds, distorts, and enslaves us. Nothing points this out, in movie form, like The Matrix. An absolute classic. If your son hasn’t watched this, now is the time. https://youtu.be/Krh1koDU2uE
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
